sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I clicked 3 purple blocks and no game over happened. Let me check what changed - did the bar increase? Did anything else happen?
The board looks the same visually. Let me check the bar state and see if clicking purple blocks does something different from clicking dots.
I need to check if the purple block clicks added to the bar, and whether there's any other effect.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after purple block clicks
if last_transition:
    bp = sum(n['pixels'] for n in last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p')
    ap = sum(n['pixels'] for n in last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p')
    bg = len([n for n in last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'])
    ag = len([n for n in last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'])
    print(f"Bar: {bp}->{ap}, Greens: {bg}->{ag}")
    
    # Check if any objects changed
    before_nodes = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    after_nodes = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    print(f"Before nodes: {len(before_nodes)}, After nodes: {len(after_nodes)}")
    
    # Check purple blocks specifically
    before_purple = [n for n in before_nodes if n['color'] == 'p' and n['pixels'] > 1]
    after_purple = [n for n in after_nodes if n['color'] == 'p' and n['pixels'] > 1]
    print(f"Large purple before: {len(before_purple)}, after: {len(after_purple)}")

# Current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
bar_purple = sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p')
print(f"Current bar purple: {bar_purple}")

# Try filling the bar completely - click all 9 dots
# Dots at row 11, cols 14,15,16,20,21,22,26,27,28
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 14},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 15},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 16},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 20},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 21},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 22},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 26},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 27},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 28}
])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"ACTION7": "Invalid", "LEFT": "Moves player left ~6 columns", "MOUSE": "Green: game over persists. Dots/purple: adds to bar.", "RIGHT": "Moves player right ~6 columns"}, "cross_level_notes": "Purple block clicks did not cause game over. Testing if filling bar completely unlocks something.", "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "Accumulates purple, 36 currently"}, "level_log": {"level2": "Purple clicks safe. Testing full bar fill."}, "win_pattern": {"level1": "Collect green blocks, move LEFT toward pink cross", "level2_hypothesis": "Fill bar completely then LEFT"}}, "level_tier": {"current_plan": "Fill bar with all 9 dot clicks, then LEFT", "failed_probes": ["Green collection - counter persists"], "goal_model": "Fill bar completely to unlock completion", "recent_findings": ["Purple block clicks safe, no game over"], "world_model": "14 greens, bar at 36 purple, 9 dots available"}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
